data.title: As a project maintainer, re-running architect-foundation preserves my existing customizations via append-only merge
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add idempotency and append-only merge logic so re-running architect-foundation preserves existing artifacts
**Why**: Without re-run safety, --auto mode or standard re-invocation could overwrite customized ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md and create duplicate ADRs
**Scope**:
- IN: Detect existing artifacts from prior runs, append-only merge (no deep merge/overwrite), diff of proposed additions shown to user, idempotency for ADRs and all generated files
- OUT: Deep merge of conflicting rules, automatic conflict resolution

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, re-invoking architect-foundation on a project with existing artifacts preserves all existing content and appends only new non-duplicate recommendations
  ← Satisfies: "the skill preserves all existing content and appends new recommendations that don't duplicate existing ones"
- When this story is complete, the user is shown a diff of proposed additions before confirmation on re-run
  ← Satisfies: "The user is shown a diff of proposed additions"
- When this story is complete, re-invocation does not create duplicate ADRs or overwrite existing generated files
  ← Satisfies: "the skill detects all existing artifacts produced by a previous run and does not duplicate or overwrite them"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Append-only merge must handle conflicting recommendations gracefully
- [Testing] Re-run idempotency needs separate test coverage per artifact type (ADRs, ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md, CLAUDE.md)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
